Class Sim

java.lang.Object
simcli.entities.actors.Sim
All Implemented Interfaces:
ISimBehaviour
Direct Known Subclasses:
NPCSim
public class Sim extends Object implements ISimBehaviour
The core actor in the simulation — a virtual person with needs, a career, an inventory, relationships, and a lifecycle governed by the State Pattern.

Each Sim tracks hunger, energy, hygiene, happiness, and social needs via a SimsNeedsTracker. Career progression is managed by CareerManager, and lifecycle transitions (Child → Teen → Adult → Elder) are handled through the LifeStage interface.

See Also:
  • LifecycleManager
  • SimsNeedsTracker

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    Sim(String name, int age, Gender gender)
    Constructs a new Sim with starting money, a random trait, and an appropriate life stage based on age.
    Sim(String name, int age, Gender gender, Job career)
    Constructs a new Sim with a pre-assigned career.

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    addItem(Item item)
    Adds an item to this Sim's inventory.
    void
    addTotalItemsBought(int amount)
    Adds to the cumulative items-bought tracker.
    void
    addTotalMoneyEarned(int amount)
    Adds to the cumulative money-earned tracker.
    void
    ageUp()
    Increments this Sim's age by one year and triggers a life-stage transition if the current stage's threshold is reached (State Pattern).
    boolean

    Checks whether this Sim's life stage permits working.
    void
    decreaseEnergy(int amount)
    Decreases this Sim's energy by the specified amount.

    Consumes a food item, restoring hunger, energy, and happiness, then removes it from the inventory.

    Returns the room this Sim is currently in, or null if not inside a residential building.

    Returns the Sim's current promotion tier within their career.

    Returns the relationship manager for this Sim, which handles all social bonds, marriages, and children.

    Returns the number of work shifts completed today.

    Returns the Sim's social need.

    getStarvingTicks()
    Returns the number of consecutive ticks this Sim has been starving.

    getState()
    Returns the Sim's current state (HEALTHY, HUNGRY, TIRED, or DEAD).

    Returns the total number of items bought by this Sim.

    getTotalMoneyEarned()
    Returns the total money earned by this Sim across all shifts.

    Returns the list of personality traits assigned to this Sim.

    Deprecated.
    Use LifecycleManager.processDay(Sim) instead.

    Checks whether the Sim possesses the given trait.
    boolean

    Returns whether the overwork warning has already been shown for this shift cycle.

    Increases this Sim's happiness by the specified amount.

    Increases this Sim's social need by the specified amount.

    Increments the Sim's days-alive counter by one.

    Increments the shift counter for today by one.
    boolean
    isChildSim()
    Returns whether this Sim was born as a child within the simulation (as opposed to being created at game start).
    boolean
    isPlayable()
    A child sim becomes playable once they reach TeenStage (age >= 13).
    void
    markAsDead()
    Marks this Sim as dead (state = DEAD).
    WorkResult
    performWork()
    Performs a work shift: drains energy/hunger/hygiene, earns salary, and has a 25% chance of promotion.
    void
    resetConsecutiveDaysMissed()
    Resets the consecutive-days-missed counter to zero.
    void
    setChildSim(boolean childSim)
    Marks this Sim as a child born in-game.
    void
    setCurrentAction(ActionState action)
    Sets the Sim's current action state.
    void
    setCurrentRoom(Room room)
    Places this Sim in the specified room.
    void
    setDaysAlive(int daysAlive)
    Sets the total number of in-game days this Sim has been alive.
    void
    setHealth(int health)
    Sets the Sim's health points directly.
    void
    setInventoryCapacity(int capacity)
    Sets the inventory capacity.
    void
    setJobTier(int tier)
    Sets the Sim's promotion tier.
    protected void
    setLifeStage(LifeStage stage)
    Sets this Sim's current life stage (State Pattern).
    void
    setMoney(int money)
    Sets the money balance of this Sim.
    void
    setName(String name)
    Sets the display name of this Sim.
    void
    setStarvingTicks(int starvingTicks)
    Sets the starving tick counter.
    void
    setWarnedAboutOverwork(boolean warned)
    Sets the overwork warning flag.
    void
    sleep(int ticksToMorning)
    Puts this Sim to sleep, restoring energy and reducing hunger proportional to the number of ticks until morning.
    void
    tick()
    Advances one game tick: decays all needs, applies cross-penalties, drains health if starving, and updates the SimState.
    void
    updateState()
    Re-evaluates the Sim's state based on current need values and updates it accordingly.

    Methods inherited from class Object

    clone, equals, finalize, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait

  • Constructor Details

    • Sim

      public Sim(String name, int age, Gender gender)
      Constructs a new Sim with starting money, a random trait, and an appropriate life stage based on age.
      Parameters:
      name - the display name of this Sim.
      age - the starting age (determines initial life stage).
      gender - the gender of this Sim.

    • Sim

      public Sim(String name, int age, Gender gender, Job career)
      Constructs a new Sim with a pre-assigned career.
      Parameters:
      name - the display name of this Sim.
      age - the starting age.
      gender - the gender of this Sim.
      career - the starting career (e.g. Job.UNEMPLOYED).

  • Method Details

    • getName

      public String getName()
      Returns the display name of this Sim.
      Specified by:
      getName in interface ISimBehaviour
      Returns:
      the Sim's name.

    • setName

      public void setName(String name)
      Sets the display name of this Sim.
      Parameters:
      name - the new display name.

    • getAge

      public int getAge()
      Returns the current age of this Sim in years.
      Returns:
      the Sim's age.

    • getGender

      public Gender getGender()
      Returns the gender of this Sim.
      Returns:
      the Sim's Gender.

    • getMoney

      public int getMoney()
      Returns the current money balance of this Sim.
      Returns:
      the Sim's money balance.

    • setMoney

      public void setMoney(int money)
      Sets the money balance of this Sim.
      Parameters:
      money - the new money balance.

    • getTraits

      public List<Trait> getTraits()
      Returns the list of personality traits assigned to this Sim.
      Returns:
      the Sim's trait list.

    • hasTrait

      public boolean hasTrait(Trait t)
      Checks whether the Sim possesses the given trait.
      Parameters:
      t - the trait to check for.
      Returns:
      true if the Sim has the trait.

    • getHunger

      public Need getHunger()
      Returns the Sim's hunger need.
      Returns:
      the Need object tracking hunger.

    • getEnergy

      public Need getEnergy()
      Returns the Sim's energy need.
      Returns:
      the Need object tracking energy.

    • getHygiene

      public Need getHygiene()
      Returns the Sim's hygiene need.
      Returns:
      the Need object tracking hygiene.

    • getHappiness

      public Need getHappiness()
      Returns the Sim's happiness need.
      Returns:
      the Need object tracking happiness.

    • getSocial

      public Need getSocial()
      Returns the Sim's social need.
      Returns:
      the Need object tracking social.

    • getState

      public SimState getState()
      Returns the Sim's current state (HEALTHY, HUNGRY, TIRED, or DEAD).
      Specified by:
      getState in interface ISimBehaviour
      Returns:
      the current SimState.

    • updateState

      public void updateState()
      Re-evaluates the Sim's state based on current need values and updates it accordingly.

    • getHealth

      public int getHealth()
      Returns the Sim's current health points (0–100).
      Returns:
      the health value.

    • getStarvingTicks

      public int getStarvingTicks()
      Returns the number of consecutive ticks this Sim has been starving.
      Returns:
      the starving tick count.

    • setStarvingTicks

      public void setStarvingTicks(int starvingTicks)
      Sets the starving tick counter. Used for save/load restoration.
      Parameters:
      starvingTicks - the number of consecutive starving ticks.

    • setHealth

      public void setHealth(int health)
      Sets the Sim's health points directly. Used for save/load restoration.
      Parameters:
      health - the health value (0–100).

    • getCareer

      public Job getCareer()
      Returns the Sim's current career.
      Returns:
      the current Job.

    • getJobTier

      public int getJobTier()
      Returns the Sim's current promotion tier within their career.
      Returns:
      the job tier (1-indexed).

    • setJobTier

      public void setJobTier(int tier)
      Sets the Sim's promotion tier. Used for save/load restoration.
      Parameters:
      tier - the job tier to set.

    • getShiftsWorkedToday

      public int getShiftsWorkedToday()
      Returns the number of work shifts completed today.
      Returns:
      shifts worked today.

    • incrementShiftsWorkedToday

      public void incrementShiftsWorkedToday()
      Increments the shift counter for today by one.

    • resetConsecutiveDaysMissed

      public void resetConsecutiveDaysMissed()
      Resets the consecutive-days-missed counter to zero. Called when the Sim successfully reports to work.

    • hasWarnedAboutOverwork

      public boolean hasWarnedAboutOverwork()
      Returns whether the overwork warning has already been shown for this shift cycle.
      Returns:
      true if the Sim has been warned about overworking.

    • setWarnedAboutOverwork

      public void setWarnedAboutOverwork(boolean warned)
      Sets the overwork warning flag.
      Parameters:
      warned - true to mark the warning as shown.

    • getInventoryCapacity

      public int getInventoryCapacity()
      Returns the maximum number of items this Sim can carry.
      Returns:
      the inventory capacity.

    • setInventoryCapacity

      public void setInventoryCapacity(int capacity)
      Sets the inventory capacity. Used when upgrading rooms or restoring saves.
      Parameters:
      capacity - the new inventory capacity.

    • getInventory

      public List<Item> getInventory()
      Returns the list of items currently held by this Sim.
      Returns:
      the Sim's inventory as a mutable list.

    • addItem

      public void addItem(Item item)
      Adds an item to this Sim's inventory.
      Parameters:
      item - the item to add.

    • getCurrentRoom

      public Room getCurrentRoom()
      Returns the room this Sim is currently in, or null if not inside a residential building.
      Returns:
      the current Room, or null.

    • setCurrentRoom

      public void setCurrentRoom(Room room)
      Places this Sim in the specified room.
      Parameters:
      room - the room to enter.

    • getCurrentAction

      public ActionState getCurrentAction()
      Returns the Sim's current action state (e.g. IDLE, WORKING, SLEEPING).
      Returns:
      the current ActionState.

    • setCurrentAction

      public void setCurrentAction(ActionState action)
      Sets the Sim's current action state.
      Parameters:
      action - the new action state.

    • getRelationshipManager

      public RelationshipManager getRelationshipManager()
      Returns the relationship manager for this Sim, which handles all social bonds, marriages, and children.
      Returns:
      the Sim's RelationshipManager.

    • getCareerManager

      public CareerManager getCareerManager()
      Returns the career manager for this Sim, which handles job state, promotions, and truancy tracking.
      Returns:
      the Sim's CareerManager.

    • decreaseEnergy

      public void decreaseEnergy(int amount)
      Decreases this Sim's energy by the specified amount.
      Parameters:
      amount - the amount of energy to drain.

    • increaseHappiness

      public void increaseHappiness(int amount)
      Increases this Sim's happiness by the specified amount.
      Parameters:
      amount - the amount of happiness to add.

    • increaseSocial

      public void increaseSocial(int amount)
      Increases this Sim's social need by the specified amount.
      Parameters:
      amount - the amount of social to add.

    • tick

      public void tick()
      Advances one game tick: decays all needs, applies cross-penalties, drains health if starving, and updates the SimState.

      Dead Sims are skipped. Non-playable child Sims only have hunger decay applied.

      Specified by:
      tick in interface ISimBehaviour

    • growOlderDaily

      @Deprecated public void growOlderDaily()
      Deprecated.
      Use LifecycleManager.processDay(Sim) instead. Retained only for backward compatibility.
      Description copied from interface: ISimBehaviour
      Called once per in-game day; handles ageing logic.
      Specified by:
      growOlderDaily in interface ISimBehaviour

    • getDaysAlive

      public int getDaysAlive()
      Returns the total number of in-game days this Sim has been alive.
      Returns:
      days-alive count.

    • setDaysAlive

      public void setDaysAlive(int daysAlive)
      Sets the total number of in-game days this Sim has been alive. Used exclusively for saving/loading the exact lifecycle state.
      Parameters:
      daysAlive - days-alive count.

    • incrementDaysAlive

      public void incrementDaysAlive()
      Increments the Sim's days-alive counter by one. Called by LifecycleManager on each day boundary.

    • markAsDead

      public void markAsDead()
      Marks this Sim as dead (state = DEAD). Called by LifecycleManager when the Sim reaches the death age threshold.

    • setLifeStage

      protected void setLifeStage(LifeStage stage)
      Sets this Sim's current life stage (State Pattern).
      Parameters:
      stage - the new LifeStage to transition to.

    • getLifeStage

      public LifeStage getLifeStage()
      Returns this Sim's current life stage.
      Returns:
      the current LifeStage, or null if uninitialized.

    • canWork

      public boolean canWork()
      Checks whether this Sim's life stage permits working.
      Returns:
      true if the Sim's current stage allows employment.

    • getCurrentStageName

      public String getCurrentStageName()
      Returns the human-readable name of the Sim's current life stage.
      Returns:
      the stage name (e.g. "Child", "Adult"), or "Unknown" if unset.

    • isChildSim

      public boolean isChildSim()
      Returns whether this Sim was born as a child within the simulation (as opposed to being created at game start).
      Returns:
      true if this is a child Sim.

    • setChildSim

      public void setChildSim(boolean childSim)
      Marks this Sim as a child born in-game.
      Parameters:
      childSim - true to mark as a child Sim.

    • isPlayable

      public boolean isPlayable()
      A child sim becomes playable once they reach TeenStage (age >= 13). Non-child sims are always playable.
      Returns:
      true if this Sim can be controlled by the player.

    • ageUp

      public void ageUp()
      Increments this Sim's age by one year and triggers a life-stage transition if the current stage's threshold is reached (State Pattern).

    • getTotalMoneyEarned

      public int getTotalMoneyEarned()
      Returns the total money earned by this Sim across all shifts.
      Returns:
      cumulative earnings.

    • addTotalMoneyEarned

      public void addTotalMoneyEarned(int amount)
      Adds to the cumulative money-earned tracker.
      Parameters:
      amount - the amount of money earned.

    • getTotalItemsBought

      public int getTotalItemsBought()
      Returns the total number of items bought by this Sim.
      Returns:
      cumulative item purchase count.

    • addTotalItemsBought

      public void addTotalItemsBought(int amount)
      Adds to the cumulative items-bought tracker.
      Parameters:
      amount - the number of items bought.

    • performWork

      public WorkResult performWork()
      Performs a work shift: drains energy/hunger/hygiene, earns salary, and has a 25% chance of promotion.
      Returns:
      a WorkResult indicating earnings, promotion, and overwork status.

    • eat

      public void eat(Consumable item)
      Consumes a food item, restoring hunger, energy, and happiness, then removes it from the inventory.
      Parameters:
      item - the consumable to eat.

    • sleep

      public void sleep(int ticksToMorning)
      Puts this Sim to sleep, restoring energy and reducing hunger proportional to the number of ticks until morning.
      Parameters:
      ticksToMorning - the number of ticks until 08:00.
